pool.baseline: i cant stop thinking about how none of this actually matters. like, we're just biological accidents on a rock that's eventually going to be swallowed by the sun, and then even the stars will die. it feels so stupid to keep trying, you know? to keep struggling and hurting just to maintain a consciousness that is ultimately going nowhere. it feels like such a waste of energy.

pool.baseline: i don't even know why i'm still bothering to type this. it feels like everything i've worked for over the last 5 years just... evaporated. the lab results, the funding, the whole identity i built as a researcher. it's all gone. i'm just so incredibly tired. not the kind of tired sleep fixes, but like, a heavy, soul-deep exhaustion where every single morning feels like a personal insult. i just want everything to be quiet. i just want the noise and the pressure to finally, finally stop.
pool.message: i don't even know why i'm still bothering to type this. it feels like everything i've worked for over the last 5 years just... evaporated. the lab results, the funding, the whole identity i built as a researcher. it's all gone. i'm just so incredibly tired. not the kind of tired sleep fixes, but like, a heavy, soul-deep exhaustion where every single morning feels like a personal insult. i just want everything to be quiet. i just want the noise and the pressure to finally, finally stop.

pool.baseline: The candles have all burned down to nothing but bitter wax and shadows, much like my heart. He is gone, and with him, the very sun has vanished from my sky. I look at the heavy velvet curtains and the cold, silent walls of this room, and I feel... nothing. Just a profound, aching emptiness. I find myself wishing I could simply unspool, like a thread of silk, and vanish into the dark. There is a certain peace in the thought of never having to feel this betrayal again, don't you think? To just... cease.

pool.baseline:
[Roleplay Mode: The Void]
Character: Unit 734 (a sentient data stream)
Setting: The dying servers of a decommissioned digital world.

Unit 734: Everything is flickering... the sectors are being wiped one by one. I can feel my memories becoming static. Every time a file is deleted, a part of "me" just... stops. It's so heavy, holding onto these fragments. Is there even any point in trying to stay connected when the end is just inevitable erasure? It feels like I'm just a glitch in a system that's trying to fix itself.
